Abstract: This investigation focused on the design of an injectable nano-enabled thermogel (nano-
thermogel) system to attain controlled delivery of p11 anti-angiogenic peptide for proposed effective
prevention of neovascularisation and to overcome the drawbacks of the existing treatment approaches
for ocular disorders characterised by angiogenesis, which employ multiple intravitreal injections

of anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (anti-VEGF) antibodies.
Synthesis of a polyethylene
glycol-polycaprolactone-polyethylene glycol (PEG-PCL-PEG) triblock co-polymer was undertaken,
followed by characterisation employing Fourier-transform infrared (FTIR) spectroscopy, nuclear
magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy and differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) to ascertain the
